rulings.Case Title: JOHN HALL JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion for summary adjudication is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression. Plaintiff¿s responses to contention interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Caterpillar Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether George Lucas, Jr. was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objection to Mr. Lucas¿ deposition on the grounds that the deposition was not completed and/or defendant did not have adequate opportunity to examine Mr. Lucas is overruled. While Mr. Lucas¿ deposition did not conclude prior to his death, defendant was afforded a meaningful opportunity to ask questions of Mr. Lucas. Mr. Lucas¿ deposition testimony, however, does not provide any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s authentication objection to exhibit C is sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Ciba Corporation, FKA CIBA SPECIALTY CHEMICALS CORPORATION, SII TO THE CHEMICALS BUSINESS OF CIBA-GEIGY CORPORATION AND SUED HEREIN AS CIBA SPECIALTY CHEMICALS CORPORATION¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not now have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence to prove their claims. By not presenting plaintiffs¿ responses to specially-prepared contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant is unable to successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, May 19, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 11. Defendant Gardena Holdings, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs¿ request for a continuance under CCP 437c(h) is denied. Mr. Hersom¿s declaration fails to provide any reasonable basis to believe that ¿facts essential to justify opposition may exist but ¿ [could not have been] presented¿ at the time plaintiffs¿ opposition papers were due. There is no indication in Mr. Hersom¿s declaration or in any other papers submitted by the plaintiffs that any of the prior depositions of defendant¿s PMK taken by the Brayton Purcell firm have been reviewed to determine if there are or are not any facts in those depositions that would assist in opposing this motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SHERFY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 19, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 18. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present any admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether John Sherfy was exposed to any asbestos disturbed or otherwise attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Riley and Mr. Ay (to the extent that he relies on Mr. Riley¿s declaration) are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN SHERFY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, MAY 19, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 18. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (exposure, false representation, and negligence) and taken off calendar as to issues 4 and 5 (strict liability and punitive damages). The declarations of Mr. Riley and Mr. Thornton create a triable issue whether John Sherfy was exposed to asbestos disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Riley and Mr. Thornton are overruled. Plaintiffs represent that they have dismissed their claim for strict liability and request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)
